articles.title: SIU clears Ottawa police officer after man breaks knee during arrest
articles.description: On Jan. 8, Ottawa police were investigating an alcohol theft when the incident occurred, according to a May 4 release by the SIU.

articles.title: Canada's 100 Best Restaurants list lauds Ottawa chef Briana Kim
articles.description: Her 12-seat Chinatown restaurant Antheia cracked the prestigious national list weeks after opening. Two other Ottawa restaurants also made the cut.

articles.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
articles.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

articles.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
articles.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

articles.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
articles.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

articles.title: Carney to take part in European summit on security, trade
articles.description: Canada is the first non-European country to attend these meetings, which have taken place twice a year since they began after Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

articles.title: Vacancies in Canada’s Senate are growing and some senators are concerned
articles.description: There are currently nine vacant seats in the Senate, with six more senators due to retire by the end of 2026. Prime Minister Mark Carney has not appointed any since taking office.

articles.title: Dr. Doug Ford takes pro-trade message to Michigan university
articles.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford received an honorary doctorate in Michigan, using his speech to warn tariffs could harm Canada-U.S. ties and urging the countries to work together.
